findingDetails.title: Proxy replays memory_save while server-side idempotency is default-off
findingDetails.description: The launcher session proxy marks memory_save replayable across backend recycle, but the memory_save receipt guard only runs when SPECKIT_MEMORY_IDEMPOTENCY is enabled and that flag is documented default-off. A backend recycle after a save mutation lands but before the response reaches the proxy can re-run the mutating save path instead of failing the pending request or requiring an enabled idempotency receipt.

noveltyJustification: One new correctness/concurrency finding in the daemon-backed CLI replay path.

ruledOut.claim: IPC socket server lacks stale-socket hardening
ruledOut.reason: The shared socket implementation checks allowed roots, socket-dir ownership/mode, symlink nodes, stale socket unlink fences, and chmods bound sockets to 0600.
The handler has receipt lookup/store logic, but it is gated by SPECKIT_MEMORY_IDEMPOTENCY.

packets.claim: launcher proxy marks memory_save replayable while server-side idempotency receipts are default-off, so backend recycle can replay a mutating save
packets.counterevidenceSought: Checked memory_save idempotency receipt lookup/store and primary logical-key duplicate guards in memory-save.ts, idempotency-receipts.ts, create-record.ts, and vector-index-mutations.ts.
packets.alternativeExplanation: Primary-row duplicate guards and receipt code reduce the impact when the feature flag is enabled or when duplicate content maps to an existing active row; however the proxy replay decision does not check the flag and the receipt guard is default-off.
packets.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if SPECKIT_MEMORY_IDEMPOTENCY is enabled by default in committed runtime configs or the proxy stops classifying memory_save as replayable unless a receipt guard is active.
